instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How Term Insurance has been affected after the COVID 19 Pandemic
Meta Description: The low-cost era of term insurance has come to an end. However, the growing awareness of the need for life and health insurance bodes well for policyholders and the industry as a whole.
Keywords: covid-19 term plan, covid-19 life insurance, online term plan
_______________________________________________________
Covid 19 effects on Insurance Industry
When the Covid 19 pandemic struck India in March 2020, general and health insurance firms were mostly unaffected in the early days, since only government-run healthcare facilities were affected.
When private institutions were authorized to treat Covid 19 patients, the number of claims skyrocketed. Rising hospitalization rates have made Indians realize the need for proper health and life insurance protection. Then, in March 2021, the terrible second wave struck, emphasizing the significance of protective clothing like never before. And, according to industry observers, this is a tendency that will continue. A look at some of the other facets of insurance that may have altered forever.
How Term Insurance was affected post the Covid 19 pandemic
The good news is that Covid 19 has raised awareness of the value of insurance. Hard-sell health insurance is no longer necessary. In the aftermath of limited or n insurance coverage, the pandemic has revealed the vulnerability of families, with numerous family members being hospitalized at the same time.
While several state governments and municipal governments set a limit on Covid 19 treatment expenses, expenditures in certain problematic situations skyrocketed. The usual hospital stay in India is 3-5 days. During the pandemic, however, numerous individuals were hospitalized for 7-10 days. In a normal case, you wouldn't start with a slight temperature and end up needing 'Plasma treatment' or ventilator support, but that's exactly what happened with Covid.
As a result, it's no surprise that getting sufficient Covid 19 term insurance was high on Indians' priority list. It doesn't matter whether you're young or old, well or sick; everyone now needs an online term plan. This pattern may or may not continue depending on the effectiveness vaccination schemes.
Even if they are already protected by their companies' group insurance, they are adopting this option. This tendency began with job losses in the early months of the pandemic. People in high-income brackets working in senior positions were just depending on their company's group policy, according to our observations.
People became aware of the degree to which medical expenditures might skyrocket, resulting in high out-of-pocket payments owing to insufficient insurance coverage or amount insured. Soon after private institutions were permitted to treat Covid 19 patients, insurance companies and hospitals clashed over treatment procedure price. Insurance companies were hesitant to cover the entire expense of PPE kits and sanitization, which hospitals said were necessary for Covid 19 treatment. Policyholders have been left in the lurch as a result of the conflicts, and have had to pay out of pocket.
As a result, they are devoting more time and effort to finding an appropriate product. During the pandemic, spending on consumables including PPE kits, gloves, and masks during hospitalization or at-home treatment on medical recommendation was significant. As a result, insurance buyers are clamoring for "all consumables to be covered" as well as domiciliary hospitalization coverages.
Term insurance firms are anticipated to provide or extend outpatient department coverage for policyholders as a result of the pandemic-induced lockdown's reliance on telemedicine and home treatment techniques.
Wrapping Up
As many people saw close relatives and friends die of the devastating illness, the desire to financially protect their own families grew throughout the epidemic. And it seems that this transformation is unstoppable. In India, the second Covid 19 wave did not spare anybody — young or old, healthy or not. As a consequence, people are more aware of the need to have enough Covid 19 term insurance coverage.
Sources:
https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/business/personal-finance/2021-review-how-health-and-term-insurance-buying-changed-completely-due-to-covid-19-7856841.html

[Mayank Saxena]: Revised, changed facts (Context: How Term Insurance has been affected after the COVID 19 Pandemic)

[Mayank Saxena]: It’s from provided source link but can be knocked off if required (Context: When the Covid 19 pandemic struck India in March 2020, general and health insura...)
[Sangeetha V/Pune HO/Product Development/Life]: This seems like a fact. Is there any data backing this? If not make it like this may continue and not will (Context: As a result, it's no surprise that getting sufficient Covid 19 term insurance wa...)
[Mayank Saxena]: Revised but can be knocked off (Context: As a result, it's no surprise that getting sufficient Covid 19 term insurance wa...)
